medical_history.chief_complaint: 7 years of slowly progressive left foot drop, and 2 years of left hand weakness and frequent cramps
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Slowly progressive left foot drop for 7 years, left hand weakness and frequent cramps for 2 years. No sensory complaints. Limped in walking, could not type with left hand. Right limbs were normal. No changes in ocular, bulbar, bladder, bowel, or respiratory function.
medical_history.family_history: No family history of neuromuscular illness

physical_examination.general_examination: Results of general physical examination were normal
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Mental status, speech, and cranial nerve functions were all normal. Strength was normal in the right limbs and in proximal left limb muscles. There was distal weakness in the distribution of the left median radial (MRC grade 4); ulnar (grade 3); peroneal (grade 2); and tibial nerves of MRC grade 4+. Tone and muscle bulk (including those of weak muscles) and sensation were normal. Tendon reflexes were normal in the right arm and leg, and in the left triceps; hypoactive in the left biceps, brachioradialis, and knee; but absent at the left ankle.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Acellular fluid with a normal protein level

other_tests.findings: Multifocal motor demyelination was evident, with PMCB in left median and ulnar nerves, prolonged distal motor latency in left peroneal and ulnar nerves, prolonged F-wave latency in all nerves so tested, with reduced conduction velocity in left median and ulnar nerves. Sensory nerve conduction studies showed normal sural, median, ulnar, and radial sensory nerve action potential (SNAP) amplitudes and conduction velocities. The sensory velocities were normal along segments that showed motor conduction block. Inching studies of motor conduction along the median nerve localized the site of PMCB to a 50-mm segment in the forearm. Sensory conduction velocities were normal in this segment.
other_tests.changes_over_time: The degree of PMCB declined after IVIg treatment

other_tests.findings: Normal cooling and vibratory thresholds in the left hand and foot
Normal myelinated fiber density, a few thinly myelinated fibers, miniature onion bulb formation, and rare fibers undergoing active demyelination. No inflammatory cells were found.
